decks.notes: The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo using Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker to create a deadly board presence. It utilizes various cards to support the combo, such as Devourer of Destiny and Kozilek's Command. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths for additional threats. The deck also includes interaction and disruption with cards like Soul-Guide Lantern and Dismember. Overall, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a lethal combination of creatures and abilities.

The main concept of this Jeskai Control deck is to maintain control of the game with a mix of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while also generating card advantage through card draw and recursion with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Snapcaster Mage. The deck also includes a mix of removal spells like Solitude and Supreme Verdict to deal with threats on the board. Overall, the key strategy is to control the game tempo and outvalue the opponent in the long run.

The deck focuses on aggression and quick damage output through cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Goblin Bombardment. It also includes cards for control and protection like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah. The deck utilizes a mix of creatures, spells, and lands to maintain pressure on the opponent and control the board state. The key strategy is to quickly build a board presence, apply pressure with aggressive creatures, and use utility cards to maintain control and finish off the opponent before they can stabilize.

The main idea of this Boros Ponza control deck is to disrupt the opponent's mana base and board state while ramping up your own resources. This is achieved through the use of land destruction spells like Cleansing Wildfire and Field of Ruin, along with targeted removal like Path to Exile and Galvanic Discharge. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Avenger's Disassembled and Price of Freedom, as well as protection cards like Solitude and Rest in Peace. Overall, the strategy focuses on control, disruption, and ultimately overwhelming the opponent with powerful plays.

This deck focuses on utilizing a combination of aggressive creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent with fast damage output. The key strategy revolves around playing efficient threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu, supported by removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Leyline Binding. The deck also includes cards like Quantum Riddler and Consign to Memory for additional card advantage and disruption. The mana base is streamlined to ensure consistent access to the necessary colors for casting spells and creatures efficiently.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quick and aggressive creature plays, backed up by strong removal and disruption spells to control the game and push damage through. Utilizing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Goblin Bombardment, the deck aims to put early pressure on the opponent and close out games quickly. The inclusion of cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Ranger-Captain of Eos provide additional value and versatility, while Ajani, Nacatl Pariah offers a powerful finisher to secure the win.

The deck focuses on utilizing Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker to generate value and control the board while setting up for a combo finish with Emrakul, the Promised End. It also includes disruption elements like Kozilek's Command and Unholy Heat to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently through cards like Ancient Stirrings and Urza's Saga, while also utilizing Blade of the Bloodchief to further enhance its creatures. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful combo finish.

The main concept of the deck is to use cost reduction effects such as Ruby Medallion and Artist's Talent to quickly generate mana and cast a high volume of spells. By chaining together rituals and cantrips like Manamorphose and Reckless Impulse, the deck aims to storm off and play a large number of spells in a single turn. This ultimately leads to a lethal Grapeshot or a powerful Past in Flames to recur key spells and continue the combo. The deck also utilizes Ral, Monsoon Mage and Wrenn's Resolve for additional card draw and spell recursion.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on overwhelming your opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle. Utilizing a mix of low-cost creatures and burn spells, the deck aims to quickly build a board presence and deal damage efficiently. The deck includes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer to apply pressure, while also using cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge to control the board. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides a strong finisher, while Ranger-Captain of Eos and Voice of Victory offer utility and resilience. Together, these cards create a strong and relentless aggro strategy.

This deck focuses on combining aggressive creatures with blinking effects to gain value and disrupt opponents. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, you can repeatedly trigger powerful abilities on creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and protect your creatures. With a mix of aggressive creatures and blinking effects, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents while maintaining control of the battlefield.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Tower) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. It then uses this mana advantage to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Thought-Knot Seer and Emrakul, the Promised End, while controlling the game with cards like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and Chalice of the Void. The deck also has access to cards like Karn, the Great Creator and All Is Dust to handle different types of threats, making it a versatile and potent control deck in the Modern format.

This deck focuses on utilizing the UrzaTron lands to quickly ramp up mana and play powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul and Thought-Knot Seer. It also includes control elements such as Ugin, Karn, and Chalice of the Void to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats while controlling the board with removal and disruption. The combination of fast mana acceleration and high-impact threats makes this deck a formidable force in Modern 1 vs 1 matchups, with a focus on resource denial and powerful finishers.

The deck focuses on playing aggressively with efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls. It also includes removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Lightning Bolt to control the board. The deck utilizes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Dalkovan Encampment to pump up creatures and push for damage. Additionally, cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ranger-Captain of Eos provide additional reach and utility. The inclusion of cards like Blood Moon and Solitude allows for flexibility and disruption against a variety of strategies. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure quickly and efficiently to overwhelm opponents.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponent's plays and controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. It aims to efficiently handle threats with removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Prismatic Ending, while also generating card advantage with Snapcaster Mage and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. Force of Negation and Counterspell provide additional protection, while Tune the Narrative offers a versatile option for both card draw and removal. The deck also includes win conditions like Wrath of the Skies and Flame of Anor to close out the game once control has been established.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard using cards like Faithless Looting and Thought Scour in order to bring back powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Abhorrent Oculus using reanimation spells like Persist and Unearth. The deck also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's game plan. The archetype of the deck is centered around combining graveyard recursion with powerful creatures, aiming to overwhelm the opponent with a big threat early in the game.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board with powerful counterspells and removal spells, while also utilizing card draw and planeswalkers to outvalue the opponent. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and slow down the pace of the game, eventually winning through card advantage and powerful finishers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Solitude. The companion Kaheera, the Orphanguard provides additional value and synergy with the deck's creature spells. The deck's mana base is designed to support the control elements and ensure consistency in casting spells.
